parsed_json.reason: Annotated page image and expected_json confirm the region is sparse title-page prose layout, not a table. pdf_oxide emitted a 15x12 table object, but the visual evidence and expected oracle unambiguously prove this is an extractor false positive with no unresolved conflict requiring human judgment.

target_evidence_used.fact: Visual evidence shows a title/prose page containing a centered 'CONTROL BASELINES' text block, hyperlinks, and publication metadata with no tabular grid or columnar structure.
Expected oracle marks type as null and text as null, confirming no table element is expected in this region.
Extractor reported row_count=15, col_count=12 over the prose region, with many empty cells and fragmented sentences, confirming a structural false positive.
Audit proved the region is sparse page/prose layout rather than a complete table, explicitly identifying this as an extractor false positive and not a human ambiguity.

validators.fact: original_page_image_path and annotated_page_image_path are present in the payload.
actual_json is non-null and contains deterministic extraction output.
Visible evidence contains continuous prose, not a table; actual_json row/column structure does not correspond to any visible tabular layout.
No visible table content exists to be cut; the bbox encompasses the page area without truncating an actual table.

parsed_json.recommended_engine_fix: Improve table-detector heuristics to avoid treating centered multi-line prose blocks and title-page layouts as tabular structures with artificial rows and columns.

fixture_candidate.reason: Clear extractor false positive on a prose title page; ideal regression test for distinguishing tables from structured text blocks.
